---
title: "Springville, Utah Solid Waste Services Study and Operational Assessment Client"
year: 1
image: /images/clackamas.jpg
image-alt: "Image of Clackamas"
categories: []
---
{{< include _return-to-pf.qmd >}}

::: {.article}

The SUVSWD hired a team including Mr. Bell in 2007 to develop a strategic solid
waste plan for its future. The impetus for this plan was a combination of
issues that required the District to consider how it would meet the demand for
solid waste services through the growth of the south Utah City region. The
issues that defined this growth included the City of Provo's rapid growth and
the City of Springville's mandate to relocate the existing transfer operations;
how the District would site and develop a new transfer station, landfill and as
well as its consideration of a waste-to-energy proposal for its waste stream.
The project team systematically reviewed and identified priorities and options
and considered an interim transfer station solution, and delivered a strategic
twenty year plan to guide the District's future.

[Thumbnail Image by Sbharris - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0](https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=24820161)
:::
